[catalogs](./genui-a2ui.a2uiprops.catalogs.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

readonly [CatalogInput](./genui-a2ui.cataloginput.md)<!-- -->\[\]

</td><td>

Components the renderer is allowed to instantiate. Each item is either a bare component (name read from `displayName ?? name`<!-- -->) or a tuple `[component, manifest]` where the manifest is the JSON the extractor emits at `dist/catalog/<Name>/catalog.json`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[className?](./genui-a2ui.a2uiprops.classname.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

string

</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Optional class name applied to the top-level `surface-${surfaceId}` view for the active surface. Use this when theme switching is expressed as surface-level classes.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[messageStore](./genui-a2ui.a2uiprops.messagestore.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

[MessageStore](./genui-a2ui.messagestore.md)

</td><td>

The raw-message buffer the developer pushes protocol messages into. `<A2UI>` subscribes via `useSyncExternalStore` and processes new messages incrementally.

The internal `MessageProcessor` (surfaces, signals, resources) is created once per mount and is \*\*not reset\*\* if `messageStore` is later replaced with a different instance. Pass a `key` prop derived from the store's identity if you want a fresh session, e.g. `<A2UI key={turnId} messageStore={turnStore} ... />`<!-- -->.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[onAction?](./genui-a2ui.a2uiprops.onaction.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

(action: [UserActionPayload](./genui-a2ui.useractionpayload.md)<!-- -->) =&gt; void

</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Called when a user action fires inside the rendered tree (button tap, etc.). Forward to your agent and push the response messages back into the same `messageStore` to update the UI. Fire-and-forget; the renderer never awaits this.

</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[renderEmpty?](./genui-a2ui.a2uiprops.renderempty.md)

</td><td>

</td><td>

() =&gt; ReactNode

</td><td>

_(Optional)_ Render before the first `beginRendering` arrives from the buffer.

</td></tr>

</td><td>

</td><td>

(err: unknown) =&gt; ReactNode

</td><td>
